Description: Ensure smooth performance in the web view with lazy loading and efficient rendering
Do: Use virtual scrolling for lists; lazy load images; minimize DOM updates; use CSS animations
Dont: Render 1000+ DOM elements; use JavaScript animations; skip lazy loading
Code Good: // Ionic virtual scroll {{ product.name }}

Description: Build custom native plugins when built-in Capacitor plugins don't cover your needs
Do: Use @capacitor/plugin-generator; implement iOS and Android separately; use TypeScript definitions
Dont: Write raw platform channel code; skip TypeScript definitions; ignore error handling
Code Bad: // No TypeScript interface\nconst result = await Capacitor.Plugins.CustomPlugin.doSomething();\n// No type safety, no documentation

Guideline: Build and Deploy with Capacitor CLI
Description: Use Capacitor CLI for building and deploying to iOS and Android stores
Do: Use npx cap sync after web builds; use npx cap open for IDE; automate with Fastlane
Dont: Manually copy web assets; skip sync; forget to update plugins
Code Good:
# Build and sync
npm run build
npx cap sync

# Open in IDE
npx cap open ios
npx cap open android

# Live reload during dev
npx cap run android --livereload --external
Code Bad:
# Manual copy
cp -r dist/ android/app/src/main/assets/public/
# Forgetting plugin sync
# Missing native dependencies

Description: Use @capacitor/preferences for key-value storage and native secure storage for sensitive data
Do: Use Preferences for simple KV; use keychain/keystore plugins for secrets; handle async properly
Dont: Use localStorage directly; store tokens in localStorage; use synchronous APIs
Code Bad: // Using localStorage - not secure, not native\nlocalStorage.setItem('auth_token', token);\n// Accessible to any web code, not encrypted
